\boxed{The Yellow Fever Epidemic of 1878 in Washington, D.C.}

Dr. Willard Bliss, the city’s health officer, was widely criticized for his mismanagement of the outbreak, including inadequate sanitation measures and misguided quarantine policies. His actions worsened the crisis, leading to hundreds of deaths. This event remains a key example of medical and administrative failures in public health history.
